transformation_type: selenium dioxide mediated α-oxidation and allylic oxidation
mechanism_type: selenium-mediated oxidation of carbonyl α-positions or allylic C-H bonds followed by hydrolysis/elimination
reactant_pattern_text: α-methylene ketone or aldehyde, or allylic/benzylic alkene with an accessible allylic C-H bond
product_pattern_text: α-1,2-dicarbonyl compound, α-keto aldehyde, allylic alcohol, or allylic enone
common_conditions: heating with SeO2 for carbonyl oxidation or catalytic SeO2/oxidant systems for allylic oxidation; regioselection depends on substitution pattern
description_short: General selenium dioxide oxidation manifold covering α-oxidation of carbonyl compounds and allylic oxidation of alkenes.

transformation_type: acid-promoted conversion of nitriles and carbocations into amides
mechanism_type: carbocation generation followed by nitrile capture to a nitrilium ion and hydrolysis
reactant_pattern_text: nitrile + alkene, tertiary alcohol, benzylic alcohol, or preformed carbocation under strong acid or Lewis acid conditions
product_pattern_text: N-alkyl carboxamide, formamide, or lactam after intramolecular trapping
common_conditions: strong acid or Lewis acid activation generates carbocations that are intercepted by nitriles and hydrolyzed on work-up
synonym_names: Ritter amidation|intramolecular Ritter reaction|modified Ritter reaction
description_short: Classical carbocation-trapping reaction in which nitriles are converted into amides under strongly acidic conditions.

transformation_type: Michael addition followed by intramolecular aldol condensation to cyclohexenones
mechanism_type: enolate conjugate addition to an enone, ring-closing aldol reaction, and dehydration
reactant_pattern_text: ketone enolate + α,β-unsaturated ketone or enone surrogate such as methyl vinyl ketone
product_pattern_text: substituted cyclohexenone or fused bicyclic enone
common_conditions: acid or base catalysis; Michael adduct formation followed by aldol ring closure and dehydration, often after isolation of the 1,5-diketone
description_short: Foundational annulation strategy that builds cyclohexenones by combining Michael addition and intramolecular aldol chemistry.

transformation_type: enantioselective allylboration and crotylboration of aldehydes
mechanism_type: six-membered chairlike allylboration transition state controlled by tartrate-derived chiral allylboronates
reactant_pattern_text: aldehyde + tartrate-derived allylboronate, crotylboronate, or homoallyl boronate
product_pattern_text: enantioenriched syn or anti homoallylic alcohol
common_conditions: low-temperature boronate addition in toluene with molecular sieves followed by aqueous work-up; syn/anti selectivity governed by boronate geometry
description_short: Tartrate-controlled allylboration method for preparing homoallylic alcohols with high enantio- and diastereoselectivity.

transformation_type: α-hydroxylation of silyl enol ethers and related enol silanes
mechanism_type: peracid epoxidation of a silyl enol ether followed by silyloxy epoxide rearrangement and hydrolysis
reactant_pattern_text: acyclic or cyclic silyl enol ether, or 2-trimethylsiloxy-1,3-diene, treated with a peracid or neutral oxidant
product_pattern_text: α-hydroxy ketone, α-hydroxy aldehyde, or α-hydroxy enone
common_conditions: oxidation at or below room temperature followed by acidic or basic hydrolysis; buffering can be important for sensitive bicyclic substrates
description_short: General peracid-based oxidation of enol silanes that converts silyl enol ethers into α-hydroxylated carbonyl compounds.
